_fixture_note: Real output from 2026-07-16 against RAW.CLUSTER_TEST_MISALIGNED (20M rows, 2.9GB) -- deliberately given an explicit clustering key on CUSTOMER_SEGMENT (a genuinely healthy key: 0.0335 average overlaps, 1.02 average depth), then queried exclusively by REGION, a different column. First live firing of reclustering_may_help: the skill correctly distinguished a healthy-but-irrelevant key from an unclustered table, recommending REGION without disparaging CUSTOMER_SEGMENT's own clustering quality.
heuristic_note: Candidate columns are found via text-matching real column names against the non-SELECT-list portion of poorly-pruned queries -- not a SQL parser. It can miss a real filter written unusually, and can't fully rule out a same-named column from a different, joined table.

advisory.detail: Table already has a clustering key (LINEAR(CUSTOMER_SEGMENT)), but 5 of 5 queries still pruned poorly. The most-mentioned filter column(s) in those queries were ['REGION'] -- worth checking whether the existing key still matches real query patterns, or whether the table needs reclustering (data drift can degrade an originally-good key over time).
